brief_justification: The only overlap is the symbol κ: in paper A it is the deformation parameter of a κ-Poincaré/κ-Galilei symmetry algebra governing noncommutative-spacetime quantum dynamics (representation theory of a deformed Lie/Hopf algebra), whereas in paper B it is a persistent-core fraction in a two-component probabilistic urn model fit to web-crawl survival and coverage rates. These are entirely different machineries — algebraic symmetry deformation versus statistical sampling/estimation — with no role-for-role correspondence, so the match is mere shared notation, not shared method.
